summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting, approving a rezoning of two Valley Lane properties from B1 (business) to R6 (high-density attached residential) for proposed duplexes. The board discussed township signage placement challenges due to gas and water mains at the preferred location near State Route 28 and Goshen Road. They also adopted an administration building use policy, approved a new service department job description, declared a 2001 Ford Ranger surplus, and appointed a new full-time zoning inspector.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for March 28 through April 10, 2023
Approved rezoning of two Valley Lane properties from B1 to R6 (Zoning Case ZC2023003)
Set special zoning workshop meeting for April 18 at 7 PM
Declared 2001 Ford Ranger (VIN ending 3471) surplus and authorized disposal
Appointed Kayla Witt as full-time zoning inspector at $19/hour with one-year probationary period
action_items: Officer Lara Item to investigate moving township sign location into parking lot area near 28 and Goshen Road, 18 inches from gas main
Chad Meadows to test parking lot asphalt depth and potentially remove a corner section for sign placement
Administrator Rose to consult with legal counsel regarding no parking signs for the parking lot
Officer Item to pursue BZA variance for sign placement before considering lot consolidation
Pine tree location to serve as Plan B for sign if primary location fails
Chief Myers to follow up with risk management on Station 18 code questions with Brian Shrive
Chad Meadows to take 2001 Ford Ranger to FIPS for scrap after fire department training
Fire department to use surplus Ford Ranger for vehicle extrication training
Kayla Witt appointment contingent upon successful pre-employment screening
key_amounts: $6,500 for one-ton truck transmission, cooler, radiator, and cooling line repairs
$35 per ton for 304 aggregate (avoided by reusing road grindings)
$1,200 approximate previous offer on 2001 Ford Ranger before engine failure
$19 per hour starting rate for new zoning inspector Kayla Witt
notable_topics: Rezoning of Valley Lane properties from B1 to R6 for duplex construction (ZC2023003)
Township signage placement challenges due to underground gas and water mains
Fillmore Construction started OPWC grant road milling and paving on Gibbs, Thompson, and McHenry
Station 18 code questions pending with risk management
Appointment of new full-time zoning inspector
Solar panel zoning authority discussion - Clermont County Township Association meeting
New township decorative signs and corp signs installation completed
Firehouse Magazine national PR feature on Goshen Township
EMS Agency of the Year recognition in Clermont Sun
attendees: Trustee Hauserman (also referred to as Halderman/Houser)
Chad Meadows (Service Department Director/Big Dan referenced separately)
Dave Jones (representing John Taylor, zoning applicant)
notes: The zoning public hearing was conducted slightly out of order - the chairperson statement and formal hearing process was read after initial discussion had already occurred, requiring staff to re-present the case synopsis. The applicant's representative Dave Jones arrived at 7 PM believing that was the hearing time due to a staffing error on the notification, but the rezoning had already been approved. An executive session was held from approximately 7:00 to 7:52 PM to discuss personnel matters under ORC 121.22G1. The board discussed upcoming Clermont County Township Association meeting regarding solar panel zoning authority, specifically townships' power over 5-49.5 megawatt installations.
title: BOT 4-11-23
